metadata.title: ASME B31.3-2018 W factor library for pipe thickness web app
metadata.intended_use: Private web app lookup library for weld joint strength reduction factor W used in ASME B31.3 pipe pressure thickness calculation.
metadata.public_github_warning: Do not publish this file in a public GitHub Pages repository unless your organization has redistribution rights. Keep ASME-derived data private or load from a protected source.
metadata.schema_policy: Top-level metadata plus formula_context, temperature_grid, w_factor_table, note_summaries, material_category_aliases, lookup_policy, reference_js_algorithm, and validation. Designed to be used with b31_3_2018_private_combined_stress_dataset.json and y_factor_library.json.
metadata.created_for: Pipe thickness calculation based on ASME B31.3

formula_context.meaning_of_W: W is the weld joint strength reduction factor. In pipe pressure design, S*E is multiplied by W for the applicable pressure-temperature condition.
formula_context.design_guardrail: W is not a substitute for longitudinal weld joint quality factor E/Ej. Use both where applicable: S * E * W.

w_factor_table.description: Carbon steel materials, including typical CS pipe grades. W is 1.0 through the listed carbon-steel temperature range in Table 302.3.5.
w_factor_table.applicability: Use for carbon steel longitudinal or spiral welded components where W is required. For temperatures below the tabulated range, use W = 1.0 where W is required. For temperatures above the listed range, designer responsibility.
w_factor_table.warning: Do not use this row beyond the listed Appendix A allowable-stress temperature limit for the selected material.

w_factor_table.applicability: Use for CrMo longitudinal or spiral welded components where W is required and applicable heat treatment/examination conditions are satisfied.
w_factor_table.warning: Do not increase W above Table 302.3.5 for CrMo using creep-test data. Confirm PWHT, filler-metal carbon content, SAW flux basicity index, and examination requirements.

w_factor_table.description: Creep Strength Enhanced Ferritic steels, normalized plus tempered condition.
w_factor_table.applicability: Use for CSEF materials such as grades 91, 92, 911, 122, and 23 when N+T heat treatment condition is applicable.
w_factor_table.warning: CSEF material selection is high-risk. Require explicit MTC and WPS/PQR review before automatic calculation. Do not increase W above Table 302.3.5 for CSEF using creep-test data.

w_factor_table.description: Creep Strength Enhanced Ferritic steels in subcritical PWHT condition.
w_factor_table.applicability: Use for CSEF longitudinal or spiral welded components with subcritical PWHT condition.
w_factor_table.warning: This row can sharply reduce allowable pressure because W falls to 0.5 over much of the range. Require engineer confirmation.

w_factor_table.group: Autogenous welds in austenitic stainless 3xx and N088xx/N066xx nickel alloys
w_factor_table.description: Autogenous welds without filler metal in austenitic stainless grade 3xx and listed austenitic nickel alloys, where solution anneal after welding is satisfied.
w_factor_table.applicability: Use only for qualifying autogenous welds without filler metal and with solution anneal after welding.
w_factor_table.warning: Do not apply this row to normal filler-metal welded A312/A358 pipe unless the autogenous weld and solution anneal conditions are proven.

w_factor_table.group: Austenitic stainless grade 3xx and N088xx nickel alloys
w_factor_table.description: Austenitic stainless steels grade 3xx and N088xx nickel alloys.
w_factor_table.applicability: Use for longitudinal or spiral welded austenitic stainless grade 3xx and N088xx nickel alloy components where W is required.
w_factor_table.warning: For high-temperature service, check heat-specific HAZ embrittlement risk and whether solution annealing of weld area is required by project specification.

w_factor_table.description: Materials other than carbon steel, CrMo, CSEF, and listed austenitic alloys.
w_factor_table.applicability: Use formula only when material is not covered by the listed steel groups and Tcr is known.
w_factor_table.warning: If temperature exceeds upper Appendix A allowable-stress temperature for the base metal, W is designer responsibility.

general_notes.a: Below the tabulated temperatures, use W = 1.0 where W is required. Above Appendix A temperature limits or outside Table 302.3.5 applicable range, W is designer responsibility.
general_notes.b: Tcr is defined as 25 degC below the temperature identifying the start of time-dependent properties in ASME BPVC Section II Part D for the base metals. For unlisted materials, Tcr is where creep-rate or stress-rupture criteria govern allowable stress. For dissimilar base metals, use the lower Tcr.
general_notes.c: Ti is the component temperature for the coincident operating pressure-temperature condition under consideration.
general_notes.d: Elevated-temperature weld life is affected by fabrication issues such as peaking at longitudinal seams and weld offset, which may increase stress and reduce service life.
general_notes.e: Linear interpolation may be used for intermediate temperature values.

note_summaries.1: CrMo steels include 1/2Cr-1/2Mo, 1Cr-1/2Mo, 1-1/4Cr-1/2Mo-Si, 2-1/4Cr-1Mo, 3Cr-1Mo, 5Cr-1/2Mo, and 9Cr-1Mo. Longitudinal and spiral seams require applicable heat treatment and examination.
note_summaries.2: Longitudinal and spiral seam fusion welded construction is not permitted for C-1/2Mo steel above 454 degC.
note_summaries.3: Weld filler metal carbon content and SAW flux basicity index requirements apply, with examination requirements.
note_summaries.5: N + T means Normalizing plus Tempering PWHT.
note_summaries.6: Applies to autogenous welds without filler metal in austenitic stainless grade 3xx and listed austenitic nickel alloys. Solution anneal after welding is required.
note_summaries.7: Alternative 100,000 hr stress rupture factors from ASME BPVC Section III, Division 1, Subsection NH may be used for specified materials and welding consumables.
note_summaries.8: Certain austenitic stainless heats can suffer HAZ embrittlement at elevated temperature. Solution annealing of the weld area mitigates susceptibility.
note_summaries.9: For other materials: W = 1.0 when Ti <= Tcr; in SI units, W = 1 - 0.00164(Ti - Tcr) for Tcr < Ti <= 816 degC. Above the base-metal Appendix A temperature limit, W is designer responsibility.

carbon_steel.description: Candidate mapping for CS pipe material descriptions. W row selection still depends on seam type and temperature.
carbon_steel.important_warning: IS-1239 and IS-3589 are only candidate material-family mappings for W factor. Do not permit full ASME B31.3 thickness calculation without approved unlisted-material allowable-stress basis.

crmo.important_warning: Confirm actual alloy family, heat treatment, seam type, WPS/PQR, filler metal, and examination before auto-calculation.

austenitic_3xx.description: Candidate mapping for austenitic stainless 3xx materials.
austenitic_3xx.important_warning: Do not use the autogenous weld row unless the weld is without filler metal and solution annealed after welding. Normal A358/A312 welded pipe generally maps to the austenitic_3xx_and_n088xx row when W is required.

csef.description: Creep Strength Enhanced Ferritic steels. Included for future expansion although not primary in current material lists.
csef.important_warning: Require explicit selection of N+T or subcritical PWHT. Never default blindly.

when_to_apply_w: Apply W only where required by ASME B31.3 para. 302.3.5(e). It applies at weld locations in longitudinal or spiral welded piping components at elevated temperature.
For seamless pipe, set W = 1.0 unless project engineering explicitly applies W to another weld location.
For occasional loads, wind, earthquake, and permissible variations, W = 1.0 and W is not required for pressure rating of the occasional/permissible variation condition.
For displacement stress allowable range SA, W is not required.
For straight-pipe internal pressure under para. 304.1.2, the product SE is multiplied by W in the denominator.

temperature_lookup.below_first_tabulated_temperature: Use W = 1.0 where W is required, but table notes for the elevated-temperature range do not apply below the tabulated range.
temperature_lookup.intermediate_temperature: Use linear interpolation between adjacent tabulated values where both values are numeric and the material row permits interpolation.
temperature_lookup.outside_applicable_range: Block automatic calculation and require designer review.
temperature_lookup.other_materials: Require Tcr. Use formula from Note 9. If Ti exceeds the upper Appendix A temperature for the base metal, block automatic calculation.

required_inputs_for_web_app: manufacturing_route_or_seam_type: seamless, ERW, EFW, SAW, LSAW, HSAW, spiral/helical, unknown
for CrMo/CSEF/austenitic high-temperature service: heat treatment and examination confirmation
block_conditions: seam type is unknown and design temperature is in elevated-temperature range
material maps to CSEF but heat treatment condition is unknown
material maps to other_materials_formula but Tcr is missing
computed temperature is outside the table/formula range
selected material is unlisted for allowable stress, such as IS-1239 or IS-3589, unless approved unlisted material basis is loaded
lookup_policy.default_recommendation: For most refinery pipe thickness calculations below creep range, W will normally be 1.0. Still retain W in the calculation engine because it becomes critical for welded pipe at elevated temperature.
